title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket successfully launched a set of Amazon's LEO satellites on July 2, marking the final satellite payload mission for this vehicle. This launch concludes the Atlas 5's role in carrying commercial satellite payloads.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's CST-100 Starliner issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's limited oversight. The technical problems have plagued the commercial crew vehicle for an extended period.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications for large and complex satellite constellations. The changes are intended to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' to handle the growing demand.

title: Unseen threats overhead: Drones endanger U.S. rocket launch sites
summary: Drones pose a significant threat to U.S. rocket launch sites, potentially causing costly delays and impacting multiple launch service providers. The issue highlights safety and operational risks in the space industry.

title: Vantor offers up-to-date imagery with WorldView 3D
summary: Vantor, formerly Maxar Intelligence, introduced WorldView 3D on July 1 to deliver high-definition and up-to-date imagery to customers. The new offering aims to enhance the currency of satellite imagery.

title: EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy
summary: EchoStar's subsidiaries in satellite TV and abandoned 5G network businesses have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. The move follows a prepackaged restructuring plan to repay debt early after selling spectrum to SpaceX and AT&T.

title: Latitude plans to conduct first launch from Oman
summary: French startup Latitude plans to conduct its first launch of a small launch vehicle from a spaceport in Oman in late 2027. This marks a significant step for the company's commercial space ambitions.

title: Blue Origin outlines new launch pad approach as it pushes to return New Glenn to flight
summary: Blue Origin plans to return its New Glenn rocket to flight by the end of the year after a pad explosion, with a rebuilt launch pad. The company outlined its new launch pad approach to recover from the incident.

title: The SpaceX IPO tells one story. Here is the more important one.
summary: The article discusses the significance of SpaceX's IPO beyond just the company's valuation and leadership, highlighting its broader implications for the space industry. It suggests that the IPO signals important trends or shifts in the sector.

title: NASA awards nearly $600 million in lunar lander missions
summary: NASA selected three companies to conduct four lunar lander missions worth nearly $600 million as part of its lunar base plans. The agency is also considering launching a spare Mars rover.

title: In 1776, the moon was a clock, a calendar and a streetlight — and it was 31 feet closer to Earth
summary: In 1776, the moon was 31 feet closer to Earth than it is today and served as a clock, calendar, and streetlight for colonists. Astronomers of the time understood its significance in navigation and timekeeping.

title: Sun fires off 10 solar flares in 24 hours as multiple Earth-bound CMEs raise northern lights hopes for July 4 weekend
summary: The Sun emitted 10 solar flares in 24 hours, sending multiple coronal mass ejections (CMEs) toward Earth. This could trigger G1-G2 geomagnetic storms, enhancing northern lights visibility around the July 4 weekend.

title: Steven Spielberg sci-fi movies ranked, worst to best
summary: A ranking of Steven Spielberg's sci-fi movies from worst to best has been published, coinciding with the release of 'Disclosure Day' in cinemas.

title: Atlas V rocket launches 29 Amazon Leo broadband satellites to orbit from Florida (video)
summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket launched 29 Amazon Kuiper broadband satellites into orbit from Florida. The mission took place early Thursday morning on July 2.

title: 'Rocket's Red Glare': How NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission celebrated America's 250th birthday
summary: NASA celebrated America's 250th birthday by painting 'America 250' on the Artemis 2 rocket. The mission marked a significant milestone in space exploration history.

title: This weird 'hot Jupiter' exoplanet has a hotspot in the wrong place, and astronomers aren't sure how
summary: Astronomers discovered a 'hot Jupiter' exoplanet with a misplaced hotspot, defying current models. The unusual finding raises questions about atmospheric dynamics in such extreme planets.

title: The growing number of satellites in orbit could soon make telescopes obsolete. 'For astronomy, this would obviously be catastrophic'
summary: The rapid increase in satellites orbiting Earth threatens to hinder ground-based telescopes, potentially making them obsolete and severely impacting astronomical research. Companies plan to launch millions more satellites, exacerbating the issue.

title: July's planetary lineup is changing — and Venus is the last one standing
summary: Venus remains the only visible planet in the evening sky this July, while Saturn, Mars, and Uranus are visible to early risers.

title: NASA will send a soccer ball to the moon —if the US wins the World Cup
summary: NASA has announced plans to send a soccer ball to the moon if the U.S. wins the FIFA World Cup. The announcement was made by agency chief Jared Isaacman on June 30.

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for July 2026, highlighting a Moon-and-planets alignment, a comet's return, Milky Way visibility, and Saturn's rings at a unique angle.

title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory reveals that the outer spiral arms of the Milky Way may extend farther than previously believed, prompting astronomers to reconsider the galaxy's structure.

title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
summary: NASA is recruiting volunteers for a yearlong simulated mission to the Moon or Mars, starting in August 2027. The study aims to gather insights into living and working in isolated interplanetary conditions.

title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A robotic servicing spacecraft named LINK, developed by Katalyst Space, is set to launch on July 2 to boost the orbit of NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. The mission marks a first-of-its-kind effort to extend the observatory's operational life.

title: NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope is studying how a planet survived the death of its star, providing insights into the future of solar systems like ours. The telescope reveals new details about a Sun-like star's expansion billions of years ago.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4934-4940: In the Land of the Polygons
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is exploring a light-toned, smooth unit on Mars, as described in a blog post by a senior research scientist. The rover's activities over sols 4934-4940 were planned in two cycles.

title: Caltech Welcomes Astrophysicist Ray Jayawardhana as New President
summary: Caltech appoints astrophysicist Ray Jayawardhana as its 10th president, succeeding Thomas Rosenbaum. The appointment was announced on January 6, with Jayawardhana holding the Sonja and William Davidow Presidential Chair and a professorship in astronomy.

title: NASA’s TESS Mission Finds Planetary System in New Way
summary: NASA's TESS mission discovered a super-Jupiter exoplanet orbiting far from its host star using ripples in space-time, a first for the mission. This finding expands TESS's capabilities beyond detecting transiting planets close to stars.

title: NASA’s Chandra Reveals ‘Red, White, Blue’ Universe for US 250th
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory released four cosmic images rendered in red, white, and blue to celebrate the U.S. 250th birthday. The images, along with new sonifications, highlight the wonders of the universe explored by the agency.

title: A Day of Flight Testing at NASA Armstrong
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center conducted flight testing to advance aviation and aerodynamic limits. The CATNLF initiative tested a wing concept to maximize smooth airflow.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar exploration missions.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following severe damage from the New Glenn anomaly. The company aims to restore operations at the site as quickly as possible.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help scientists study galactic structures and star formation.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX completed a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate visitors about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday morning using a Falcon 9 rocket. This test marks a significant step in the development of SpaceX's reentry technology.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. The busy month for the crew includes maintenance and operational tasks.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and key priorities. These events aim to engage the industrial community and provide updates on follow-up activities.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A breakthrough in robotic arm technology is announced, promising to revolutionize space operations. The innovation is expected to enhance satellite servicing and spacecraft assembly.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science research by involving skilled research institutes in space missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and validating materials for ESA's missions to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these researchers.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC, beating the 72-hour deadline.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, Aurora. The mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has revealed plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to use lunar dust to build support structures for reactor housing and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA praised Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and announced contracts for moon base missions. The agency plans to use updated versions of existing lander designs for frequent lunar landings with NASA payloads.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It highlights the progress and objectives of these missions under NASA's CLPS initiative.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. The collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the space applications sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from SSC Space’s Esrange Space Center in Sweden by 2028 under a new franchise model. This marks Firefly's expansion into international launch markets.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing operational risks as NASA approaches the 2030 extension date, with the risk margin for regular operations described as 'alarming' by a NASA advisory committee chair.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the applications dominating satellite traffic in space and their significance beyond government use. It highlights the growing importance of non-government satellite networks as thousands of new satellites are launched.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations after spectrum transactions. The move aims to restructure the company's financial obligations.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO country for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president to oversee daily operations and expand manufacturing capabilities. Campbell will focus on growing the company's production and investment in the space industry.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.

title: C-Band Round 2: FCC Set to Vote on Another C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC is set to vote in July on auctioning 160 MHz of the upper C-band spectrum. This follows the first C-band clearing process.

title: Thales Alenia Space Wins Software-Defined Satellite Order from Es’hailSat
summary: Thales Alenia Space secured a software-defined satellite contract from Es’hailSat for the Eshail-3/Türksat-Biruni satellite, which will provide high-speed services. Es’hailSat is a leading Middle Eastern satellite operator.

title: New Megaconstellation Startup Eclipse Space Targets Sovereign Market
summary: Eclipse Space, a new megaconstellation startup founded by SpaceX alumni, has emerged from stealth mode to target sovereign governments and enterprises for constellation ownership.

title: Saudia and Neo Space Group Linefit Inaugural Airbus A321XLR Aircraft With IFC
summary: Saudia and Neo Space Group have inaugurated the first line-fit activation of NSG Skywaves In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) on Saudia's Airbus A321XLR aircraft. The system is powered by NSG Skywaves and utilizes SES's satellite infrastructure.
